meaning: n.哑巴,傀儡,假人,假货adj.虚拟的,假的,虚构的n.[计] 哑元
examples: Dummy patrol cars will be set up beside motorways to frighten speeding motorists.
The device adopted a five-dummy-plate four-post framework.
The other bag is just a dummy.
However, the changes took longer to appear in those patients who responded to the dummy pill.
Of course, Mance is no dummy and asks why Jon wants to join them.
